idea: Add a backend endpoint for health checks

business_requirements: Develop a system that enables communication between agents.
Ensure secure and reliable message exchange between agents.
user_stories: As an agent, I want to send messages to other agents so that we can collaborate effectively.
As an agent, I want to receive messages from other agents to stay informed and respond accordingly.
As a system administrator, I want to monitor agent communications to ensure proper functioning and security.
edge_cases: Agents attempting to communicate when one or more agents are offline.
Handling message delivery failures or delays.
Preventing unauthorized agents from accessing communication channels.
Managing message conflicts or simultaneous communications between multiple agents.

business_requirements: Develop a system where agents can communicate with each other effectively.
Ensure secure and reliable message exchange between agents.
user_stories: As an agent, I want to send messages to another agent so that we can collaborate on tasks.
As an agent, I want to receive messages from other agents to stay informed and respond accordingly.
As a system administrator, I want to monitor agent communications to ensure proper functioning and security.
edge_cases: Agents attempt to communicate when one is offline or unreachable.
Messages are lost or delayed during transmission.
Agents send malformed or unexpected message formats.
High volume of messages causing system overload or delays.

business_requirements: Develop a system where agents can communicate with each other effectively.
Ensure secure and reliable message exchange between agents.
user_stories: As an agent, I want to send messages to another agent so that we can collaborate on tasks.
As an agent, I want to receive messages from other agents to stay informed and respond accordingly.
As a system administrator, I want to monitor agent communications to ensure proper functioning and security.
edge_cases: Agents attempt to communicate when one is offline or unreachable.
Messages are lost or delayed during transmission.
Agents send malformed or unexpected message formats.
High volume of messages causing system overload or delays.

tasks.description: Design and implement a messaging protocol for agent-to-agent communication ensuring message integrity and security.
acceptance_criteria: Agents can send and receive messages using the protocol.
Messages are encrypted and authenticated.
Protocol handles malformed messages gracefully.

tasks.description: Develop a message routing and delivery system that ensures reliable message exchange even if agents are offline or unreachable.
acceptance_criteria: Messages are queued and delivered when agents come online.
System retries sending messages on failure.
No message loss under normal operation.

tasks.description: Implement monitoring and logging for all agent communications to allow system administrators to track message flow and detect anomalies.
acceptance_criteria: All messages are logged with timestamps and sender/receiver info.
Administrators can query logs for specific agents or time periods.
Alerts are generated for suspicious activities or failures.

tasks.description: Create scalability mechanisms to handle high volumes of messages without system overload or delays.
acceptance_criteria: System maintains performance under high message load.
Load balancing or message throttling is implemented.
No message loss or significant delay during peak usage.

input.description: Design and implement a messaging protocol for agent-to-agent communication ensuring message integrity and security.
acceptance_criteria: Agents can send and receive messages using the protocol.
Messages are encrypted and authenticated.
Protocol handles malformed messages gracefully.

changes.diff:
--- a/messaging/protocol.py
+++ b/messaging/protocol.py
@@ -0,0 +1,69 @@
+import json
+import base64
+import os
+from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.aead import AESGCM
+from cryptography.exceptions import InvalidTag
+
+class MessageProtocolError(Exception):
+    pass
+
+class MessageProtocol:
+    """Messaging protocol for agent-to-agent communication with encryption and authentication."""
+
+    def __init__(self, key: bytes):
+        """
+        Initialize the protocol with a symmetric key.
+        :param key: 32-byte key for AES-256-GCM
+        """
+        if not isinstance(key, bytes) or len(key) != 32:
+            raise ValueError("Key must be 32 bytes")
+        self.key = key
+        self.aesgcm = AESGCM(key)
+
+    def encrypt_message(self, message: dict) -> str:
+        """
+        Encrypt and authenticate a message dictionary.
+        :param message: dict to send
+        :return: base64 encoded string of nonce + ciphertext + tag
+        """
+        try:
+            plaintext = json.dumps(message).encode('utf-8')
+        except (TypeError, ValueError) as e:
+            raise MessageProtocolError(f"Message serialization failed: {e}")
+
+        nonce = os.urandom(12)  # 96-bit nonce for AESGCM
+        ciphertext = self.aesgcm.encrypt(nonce, plaintext, None)
+        # ciphertext includes the tag at the end
+        # final message: nonce + ciphertext
+        combined = nonce + ciphertext
+        encoded = base64.b64encode(combined).decode('utf-8')
+        return encoded
+
+    def decrypt_message(self, encoded_message: str) -> dict:
+        """
+        Decrypt and verify a received message.
+        :param encoded_message: base64 encoded string
+        :return: original message dict
+        :raises MessageProtocolError on failure
+        """
+        try:
+            combined = base64.b64decode(encoded_message)
+            if len(combined) < 12 + 16:  # nonce + tag minimum
+                raise MessageProtocolError("Message too short")
+            nonce = combined[:12]
+            ciphertext = combined[12:]
+            plaintext = self.aesgcm.decrypt(nonce, ciphertext, None)
+            message = json.loads(plaintext.decode('utf-8'))
+            if not isinstance(message, dict):
+                raise MessageProtocolError("Decrypted message is not a dict")
+            return message
+        except (InvalidTag, ValueError, json.JSONDecodeError) as e:
+            raise MessageProtocolError(f"Message decryption or parsing failed: {e}")
+
+    def send_message(self, sender, receiver, message: dict):
+        """
+        Send a message from sender to receiver using the protocol.
+        :param sender: sending agent instance with a receive_message method
+        :param receiver: receiving agent instance
+        :param message: dict message
+        """
+        encoded = self.encrypt_message(message)
+        receiver.receive_message(encoded, sender)
+
+    def receive_message(self, encoded_message: str, sender):
+        """
+        Receive and decrypt a message.
+        :param encoded_message: base64 encoded string
+        :param sender: sending agent instance
+        :return: dict message
+        """
+        try:
+            message = self.decrypt_message(encoded_message)
+            return message
+        except MessageProtocolError as e:
+            # Handle malformed or tampered messages gracefully
+            # Log or notify as needed (omitted here)
+            return None
+
changes.rationale: Implemented a MessageProtocol class using AES-256-GCM for encryption and authentication. It provides methods to encrypt, decrypt, send, and receive messages. The protocol handles malformed messages gracefully by catching exceptions and returning None on receive. This satisfies the acceptance criteria for secure, authenticated agent-to-agent communication.

--- a/messaging/protocol.py
+++ b/messaging/protocol.py
@@ -0,0 +1,69 @@
+import json
+import base64
+import os
+from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.aead import AESGCM
+from cryptography.exceptions import InvalidTag
+
+class MessageProtocolError(Exception):
+    pass
+
+class MessageProtocol:
+    """Messaging protocol for agent-to-agent communication with encryption and authentication."""
+
+    def __init__(self, key: bytes):
+        """
+        Initialize the protocol with a symmetric key.
+        :param key: 32-byte key for AES-256-GCM
+        """
+        if not isinstance(key, bytes) or len(key) != 32:
+            raise ValueError("Key must be 32 bytes")
+        self.key = key
+        self.aesgcm = AESGCM(key)
+
+    def encrypt_message(self, message: dict) -> str:
+        """
+        Encrypt and authenticate a message dictionary.
+        :param message: dict to send
+        :return: base64 encoded string of nonce + ciphertext + tag
+        """
+        try:
+            plaintext = json.dumps(message).encode('utf-8')
+        except (TypeError, ValueError) as e:
+            raise MessageProtocolError(f"Message serialization failed: {e}")
+
+        nonce = os.urandom(12)  # 96-bit nonce for AESGCM
+        ciphertext = self.aesgcm.encrypt(nonce, plaintext, None)
+        # ciphertext includes the tag at the end
+        # final message: nonce + ciphertext
+        combined = nonce + ciphertext
+        encoded = base64.b64encode(combined).decode('utf-8')
+        return encoded
+
+    def decrypt_message(self, encoded_message: str) -> dict:
+        """
+        Decrypt and verify a received message.
+        :param encoded_message: base64 encoded string
+        :return: original message dict
+        :raises MessageProtocolError on failure
+        """
+        try:
+            combined = base64.b64decode(encoded_message)
+            if len(combined) < 12 + 16:  # nonce + tag minimum
+                raise MessageProtocolError("Message too short")
+            nonce = combined[:12]
+            ciphertext = combined[12:]
+            plaintext = self.aesgcm.decrypt(nonce, ciphertext, None)
+            message = json.loads(plaintext.decode('utf-8'))
+            if not isinstance(message, dict):
+                raise MessageProtocolError("Decrypted message is not a dict")
+            return message
+        except (InvalidTag, ValueError, json.JSONDecodeError) as e:
+            raise MessageProtocolError(f"Message decryption or parsing failed: {e}")
+
+    def send_message(self, sender, receiver, message: dict):
+        """
+        Send a message from sender to receiver using the protocol.
+        :param sender: sending agent instance with a receive_message method
+        :param receiver: receiving agent instance
+        :param message: dict message
+        """
+        encoded = self.encrypt_message(message)
+        receiver.receive_message(encoded, sender)
+
+    def receive_message(self, encoded_message: str, sender):
+        """
+        Receive and decrypt a message.
+        :param encoded_message: base64 encoded string
+        :param sender: sending agent instance
+        :return: dict message
+        """
+        try:
+            message = self.decrypt_message(encoded_message)
+            return message
+        except MessageProtocolError as e:
+            # Handle malformed or tampered messages gracefully
+            # Log or notify as needed (omitted here)
+            return None
+
changes.rationale: Implemented a MessageProtocol class using AES-256-GCM for encryption and authentication. It provides methods to encrypt, decrypt, send, and receive messages. The protocol handles malformed messages gracefully by catching exceptions and returning None on receive. This satisfies the acceptance criteria for secure, authenticated agent-to-agent communication.
